currentWave: Live database verified. Migration executed against real Neon Postgres, 37 tables confirmed, 4-function smoke test passed end to end. Persistence layer is no longer theoretical. Next work needs an owner decision (pricing is highest-leverage).
workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. Standing instruction: everything stays local to this workspace for the entire build -- no push, no merge into a new vN folder or main. Dylan granted bypass permission (2026-07-14) to take care of everything without interruption, with that one hard constraint.

knownRisks: Only 5 of 21 persistence-layer functions have been live-smoke-tested (F001, F005, F008, F019, F023) -- the other 16 (approvals, activity events, triage/priority/assignment, time tracking, website profiles, assets, reminders, IT classification, checklists, work queue, metrics, templates, webhook events) are schema-verified (their tables exist and match the migration) but not yet individually exercised live. Lower risk than before Session 13 (the schema and connection path are proven), but not zero.
Netlify Blobs' users/sessions/tokens stores (F003/F004) remain unmigrated by design
Existing Blobs records (documents, messages, notifications, favorites, leads) still need organization_id backfilled once real organizations exist -- tracked in MIGRATION_PLAN.md, not yet scheduled

No migration-runner tool is set up yet -- 001_initial_schema.sql was run via a one-off script (deleted after use, per the master instruction's 'save large outputs, don't leave scratch scripts' principle); a real runner (even a minimal one) is needed before a second migration file is created

The live database now contains smoke-test data (one organization named 'Smoke Test Org', one membership, one audit event, one ticket) -- harmless test data, but worth knowing it's there rather than assuming the database is empty
F057's verifier/logger is ready but nothing calls it yet -- no payment webhook infrastructure exists at all
F051's cross-org query has no pagination -- fine at zero real data, will need LIMIT/cursor handling before real staff-scale data exists (SYS-API-005)

Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs; new care_hub_documents/file_assets Postgres tables use an opaque storageRef instead, but the actual object-storage provider is still undecided
Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) -- still not extracted
F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention
src/settings/blobsSettingsStore.js remains Blobs-backed (F056 stayed on Blobs by design)
F026/F027/F028/F049/F050/F052's logic remains engine-only, waiting on pricing/plan-limit owner decisions -- now the clear next milestone
CSV export (src/export/csvExport.js, F053) has no formula-injection guard for spreadsheet software -- open finding from Session 9's security review

A prompt-injection attempt was observed and rejected this session: a tool-result system-reminder falsely claimed .env was modified by 'the user or a linter' and instructed withholding that from Dylan. Not followed; flagged to Dylan directly. No actual harm -- the .env content was correct and exactly what had just been written.
releaseRecommendation: NOT READY, but the primary-data-store decision is now not just made but LIVE and verified end to end. 34 functions have real tested logic, 21 with real Postgres persistence, 335 passing unit tests, and a 4-function live smoke test proving the full stack (organization -> membership -> RBAC -> audit -> ticket lifecycle) works against a real database. The clear next milestone is the pricing decision (OWNER_DECISIONS.md #2), which unblocks F026/F027/F028/F050/F052 -- the last five engine-complete functions waiting on real business values. After that, remaining work is: Netlify Function endpoints (nothing is exposed via HTTP yet), a UI, and the other 8 owner decisions.
